title: Combine zero address check with left-shift packing — one operation serves two purposes
trigger_patterns: check receiver is not zero address
pack address into upper bits and validate
zero address guard before storage write
description: shl(96, receiver) shifts the address into the upper 160 bits for packing. If receiver is address(0), shl(96, 0) = 0. A single iszero(packed) check catches both cases: zero address AND confirms the shift succeeded. No separate address validation needed — the packing operation itself is the validation.
pattern_before:
require(receiver != address(0), 'zero receiver');
uint256 packed = (uint256(receiver) << 96) | feeNumerator;
when_to_apply: Any function that packs an address into upper bits and must reject the zero address
when_not_to_apply: When address needs to stay in lower 160 bits, or when zero address is a valid input
